title: Arity is grade, not a counted feature
body: Corrected my proposal to register 'arity/N' as a primitive K. The correct framing: arity is the grade of a wedge discovered via σ/τ correspondence between grade-(N-1) and grade-1 (or grade-N-1 things). N is not tracked in a variable; it is structurally evolved via iterated wedge passes. This correction supersedes the 'register arity as primitive' design direction.

title: Lowercasing is hardcoded equivalence, not a generic transform
body: I had framed the lowercase normalization as 'a generic casing normalization, not a hand-picked mapping'. The user corrected that this is still a hardcoded mapping at a higher level. Reverted. The correct rule is strictly no canonicalization: all observational distinctions are preserved verbatim at registration.

title: Even 'elaborate subtree matching for specific axioms' must emerge
body: I briefly proposed writing pattern-matchers for axiom structures. User corrected this must ALSO be emergent, not bespoke. The principle extends to every level: no recognizer for any specific pattern (not kinds, not arity, not axioms, not source-clusters, not triples) may be hand-written. All recognition is κ-articulated.

title: Alignment is the evidence distribution itself, not a Boolean gate
body: I proposed interpretation (b): alignment = pair demonstrates Boolean across some K-pair. User corrected: this is still a commit gate in disguise. Pre-Boolean evidence is valuable signal — κ-evolution FEEDS on it; it is not a waystation on the road to Boolean. Boolean-earning is an occasional reading of the Belnap distribution, not its point. Commit predicates at any level are out.

title: '/' in (σ/τ) is matrix-cell separator, not ratio
body: I interpreted ((σ/τ)/(τ/σ)) as a division / ratio / Jaccard-style operator. User corrected: each '/' separates left from right columns in a four-valued logic matrix. Boolean is earned when a flag fires while the other does not AND the other flag fires while the first does not — both off-diagonal cells populated. This is XOR-independence, computed trivially as two cell counts both > 0.

title: Session context externalized to design/ SPPF
body: Sessions kept hitting context limits trying to carry the full history of principles + decisions + corrections + code state in one working set. Moved the design history into design/ as JSONL files walkable by scripts/design_sppf.py. Future sessions load only the slice they need (specific principles, dependents of a decision, open questions) rather than the conversation.

title: Open questions resolve via maximal freedom, not specific picks
body: I was treating the five open questions (q-tau-sigma-wedge-combinator, q-wedge-articulation-signal, q-weight-gradient-direction, q-report-format-for-belnap-distribution, q-smoke-test-scale) as blockers needing concrete user decisions. Under p-maximal-freedom they collapse to a single non-answer: don't pick, parameterize. Implement the algebra uniformly; make scorers, objectives, and views pluggable; default to the most symmetric initial state; allow asymmetry as a free move at higher grades. Each question is thus closed by deferral-to-framework rather than resolved by commitment.

title: Metadata class was a smuggled taxonomy, removed in Stage 2.6
body: Stage 2.5 introduced a Metadata dataclass (display, source_path, line, extra) as a 'separate informational-only struct the algebra never inspects'.  User pointed out: 'what even is metadata in this context?'  On reflection, Metadata as a category is itself a privilege the framework shouldn't grant.  Under p-source-is-a-k and p-no-bespoke-recognition, there is no reporting-vs-structure dichotomy: every observable is a K.  If an extractor wants display labels or source paths or line numbers visible to reports, it registers them as atomic K's (e.g. Atom('source:paper/x.py')) that fire on the Thing's tau bitmap.  Reports are queries over the pool + bitmaps, not queries over a separate metadata dict.  Stage 2.6 removes the Metadata class, the State.metadata field, metadata_of/metadata_dict accessors, and all metadata handling in merge/restrict.  Thing is now strictly (ThingId, tau_mask, sigma_mask).

title: Stage 3.5: bijective K keys; stable position-based anon-thing-IDs; surfaced parse errors
body: Stage 3 audit found three smells that combine to violate the newly-explicit p-bijective-hash-consing principle. Fix: (1) K.key() now derives from K.structure() — a bijective tuple serialized via JSON. ('atom', observable), ('wedge', left.structure(), right.structure()), ('zero',). JSON encoding is round-trippable; observables containing commas or parens no longer produce ambiguous wedge keys. (2) Anonymous-thing IDs now use (source_path, source_line) positional disambiguation instead of an extraction-order counter. Every adapter (PandocNode, TreeSitterAgdaNode, PyAstNode) already exposes source_path and source_line. Collision detection: extract_initial_state raises ValueError if two Things get the same ID (would indicate line-level collision requiring byte-level disambiguation). (3) Parse failures are logged to stderr (per-file) rather than silently swallowed. They remain non-fatal for corpus-wide extraction but are now visible.

title: Stage 3.6: surface all identity components as probes, not just as ID-string text
body: Stage 3 audit under the newly-explicit p-yoneda-identity found that Thing identity components (source, top-level kind, path, name) were encoded only as opaque text in ThingId strings.  This violates p-yoneda-identity (identity = probe profile) and p-probes-are-half-spaces (discriminable info must live in the probe algebra).  Stage 3.6 expands the initial atom pool to include: kind_at_root:{kind} (distinct from subtree-kind probes), path:{component} per path fragment, name:{verbatim} per named thing's verbatim name.  NOT added: line numbers (positional disambiguation only, not compositionally meaningful yet), name-token splits (canonicalization, forbidden by p-no-canonicalization), docstring content (docstrings are their own things per p-thing-is-not-its-relations).  Scale: ~145 → ~1100+ atoms; still well within bitmap feasibility.

title: Stage 4.5: four-cell scoring is XOR-of-columns; overlap cell demands articulation
body: Stage 4's scorer_entropy_of_four_cell computed Shannon entropy across all four cells including n_00 (gap) and n_11 (overlap), conflating gap-as-absence with overlap-as-redundancy under a single categorical-distribution reading.  User specified the correct semantics: the score of a four-cell is the XOR of the two columns — only off-diagonals score.  n_00 is gap (zero score, zero trigger).  n_11 has zero score but is the deterministic trigger for wedge articulation when the joint is not yet in the pool.  Stage 4.5 adds scorer_xor_off_diagonal as the principled primary scorer (n_01 + n_10); retains scorer_entropy_of_four_cell as a general-distribution statistic for reporting / characterization but not as a ranking default.  Also adds scorer_xor_off_diagonal_log_pair for the Boolean-earning variant (log(n_01) + log(n_10) when both populated).  The overlap-demand rule (p-overlap-demands-wedge-articulation) will be enforced in Stage 5's step() function.

title: Stage 4.6: structural scorer/objective classes; split two-orientation oracle scorer; decomposed signatures
body: Stage 4 used bare Callable type aliases and closure-based composites.  Under p-functions-have-structural-identity (added this session) closures must become frozen dataclasses with explicit ``structure`` properties.  Stage 4.6 delivers: (1) Scorer and Objective as Protocol classes with decomposed named-parameter signatures; ScoreValue and ObjectiveValue as NewType float wrappers for semantic decomposition.  (2) Each scorer refactored from bare function to frozen dataclass with __call__ and structure.  Singleton instances exposed under the old function names for call-site continuity.  (3) CompositeScorer and CompositeObjective as frozen dataclasses containing tuple of (weight, component) pairs; identity is structural (recursive tuple).  (4) ``scorer_oracle_boolean_witness`` split into two orientation-specific scorers (tau-sigma vs sigma-tau) per p-tau-sigma-not-opposite.  Union variant provided as a composite for callers who want the sum.  (5) ``import math`` moved to module top.  Per-K firing-bitmap precomputation deferred to Stage 5 (efficiency concern, not Stage 4.6 scope).

title: WeightUpdater stub removed; weight updates reinterpreted as new-K articulation
body: Stage 5 scaffolded a pluggable WeightUpdater protocol with a NoOpWeightUpdater default per d-weight-objective-pluggable.  User clarified that weight updates as a concept destroy information — the mutation replaces the old scalar and loses the regime in which earlier wedges were articulated.  The honest alternative is p-weight-update-is-new-k-articulation: instead of modifying a K's weight, articulate a NEW K with the same observable and a different scalar.  Both coexist.  Stage 5.5 removes the WeightUpdater parameter from step(), removes NoOpWeightUpdater and the WeightUpdater Protocol, and leaves the State.weights field in place (now set-once per K at articulation time, not mutated by step()).  d-weight-objective-pluggable is superseded by p-weight-update-is-new-k-articulation in its 'weights tune toward objective' reading; the 'objectives exist' part remains valid as read-out and K-articulation-driver signal.

title: Stage 5.5: no-default articulation budget; WeightUpdater removed; σ channel exercised
body: Stage 5 audit identified four addressable concerns.  Stage 5.5 fixes: (1) max_articulations_per_step default changes from 100 to None meaning 'all demanded wedges articulated in one step'.  Bounded budgets become a caller choice, not a framework default — matching p-maximal-freedom (don't pick) and p-closed-self-referential-loop (no external schedule baked in).  (2) WeightUpdater removed per c-weight-updater-becomes-new-k-articulation.  (3) _articulate_wedge_into_state docstring updated with the 'wedge of appearance vs reality' pattern: explicit note that the function APPEARS pure but MUTATES step-local caches as an incrementalization strategy scoped to the batch.  (4) σ-channel path exercised in smoke test: _compute_firing_bitmaps called with channel='sigma' and result compared to the τ path; under the current τ=σ regime they agree, but the path itself is tested.

title: Stage 7.0: eager numpy refactor of internal representation
body: Thing.tau_mask and sigma_mask are Python ints (arbitrary-precision bitmasks).  Stage 7.0 converts them to np.ndarray(dtype=bool) throughout.  Affected surface: Thing (new __post_init__ for array immutability; __hash__ via .tobytes(); new remap() using numpy index-transform), _compute_firing_bitmaps (returns 2D bool array [pool_size, n_things] instead of dict[int, int]), _count_four_cell (numpy bitwise ops, sum for popcount), step() (batched articulation — all new wedges computed as 2D firing matrix, concatenated onto every thing's mask once per batch instead of once per wedge), extract_initial_state (constructs Things with np arrays from the start).  Signature() continues to work via Thing.__hash__ which uses .tobytes() for deterministic content hashing.  Smoke tests adjusted for numpy-array assertions.  Under p-refactor-debt-paid-eagerly: the scope is manageable now (1087 atoms × 854 things), and paying now prevents the surface from growing through Stages 7.1+.

title: Stage 7.1: hybrid JSONL + HDF5 I/O with generator run()
body: Stage 7.1 delivers persistence: pool/trajectory/oracle as append-only JSONL; per-thing τ/σ bitmaps as HDF5 datasets with resizable axes (thing count + pool bit count).  dump_state(state, out_dir) writes both.  load_state(in_dir) reconstructs the State.  Generator form of run_to_fixed_point (run_iter) yields State at each iteration for progress inspection / interruptible runs / automatic per-iteration checkpoint — addressing the Stage 6 audit's ergonomics concern.  Refines d-report-is-full-state-jsonl via p-storage-matches-data-shape: the 'full state queryable' intent stands; the 'single-format JSONL' implementation is now hybrid.

title: Stage 7.0.5: apply numpy arrays array-natively, not via Python loops
body: Stage 7.0 installed np.ndarray as the representation but left three inner-loop code paths in Python: (1) _count_four_cell looped per-thing and appended to Python lists before converting to numpy; (2) step()'s candidate-pair enumeration did a Python C(n, 2) loop per thing × n_things things; (3) Thing.__hash__ re-hashed .tobytes() every call, compounding through signature() twice per iteration in run_to_fixed_point.  Under p-numpy-is-the-natural-cpu-representation the principle says 'arrays for application to other arrays' — arrays stored but not applied is neither-fish-nor-fowl.  Stage 7.0.5: (1) _count_four_cell accepts optional firing_bitmaps for fast path via 2D matrix slicing + np.sum; Scorer protocol extends with optional firing_bitmaps kwarg so scorers can take the fast path when called from step(); (2) step() candidate enumeration uses firing_bitmaps @ firing_bitmaps.T to build a [pool_size × pool_size] co-fire count matrix, then extracts upper-triangle pairs where count > 0 — O(P²·N) BLAS matmul replaces Python O(P·N·bits²); (3) Thing.__hash__ caches the computed hash via object.__setattr__ on first call, read via getattr on subsequent.  No principle change; all active principles preserved.

title: Stage 7.0.6: unmaterialize Thing._hash and replace signature()-based fixed-point with trajectory signal
body: User observation: 'we're materializing something that shouldn't be materialized in the first place'.  Stage 7.0.5 added Thing._hash caching to optimize signature() comparison in run_to_fixed_point.  But signature() returns a tuple; tuple == tuple uses element-wise __eq__, not hash().  Thing.__hash__ is never invoked during fixed-point detection.  The cache solved a phantom problem AND introduced cross-process staleness for no benefit.  Stage 7.0.6 reverts the cache: Thing.__hash__ returns hash((id, shape, tobytes(), tobytes())) on every call, pure function of content; no state, no cross-process issue.  Cascading: run_to_fixed_point no longer computes or compares signatures.  Termination = state.trajectory[-1]['articulated_count'] == 0.  Direct signal from the trajectory (which step() already writes), exact match for d-fixed-point-is-termination's 'no productive wedge articulation' criterion.  signature() itself is retained for diagnostic / external equality checks (smoke-test assertions, comparison across saved states) but is no longer in the closed-loop hot path.

title: p-type-theory-everywhere reframed: primary focus is tensor shape, not class shape
body: User observation: 'Instead of a type making assertion about the obligations of the shape of a class, it should make assertions about the obligations of the shape/properties of a tensor.'  p-type-theory-everywhere was originally phrased around class-shaped types (K as inductive, TauSigma as F₂², Scorer as Protocol).  Stage 7's eager numpy refactor moved the algebra to tensor-valued operation; the primary type information shifted with it but the principle's body didn't follow.  c-type-theory-reframed-via-tensor-shape adds p-types-are-tensor-shape-obligations to carry the tensor-shape reading; p-type-theory-everywhere retains its class-shaped reading as secondary scaffolding.  Practical consequence for Stage 7.1: TrajectoryEntry is NOT a frozen dataclass — it's a row of a structured-dtype numpy array; the dtype schema IS the type.  Thing's tau_mask / sigma_mask should gain shape annotations (Bool[Array, 'pool_size']).  firing_bitmaps, ScoreValue, and other tensor-valued quantities similarly get shape-typed.

title: Stage 7.0.7: Pool + State.things + hash-consing refactored to numpy-native shapes (three-lemma bundle)
body: Stage 7.0.7 enacts three coupled lemmas as one refactor cycle: (a) l-pool-as-structured-dtype-array in 7.0.7a — Pool's authoritative representation becomes a 1-D structured-dtype numpy array (POOL_DTYPE = [('key','O'),('grade','u1')]) with a _key_to_bit dict cache for O(1) lookup; public .keys and .by_key properties preserve legacy shape for unchanged callers; (b) l-state-things-as-parallel-arrays in 7.0.7b — State stores thing_ids + tau_masks + sigma_masks as parallel 1-D/2-D arrays; 'a Thing' becomes a row-view reconstructed on demand via the .things property; merge's pool-remap is vectorized via advanced indexing; firing_bitmaps_of(state) returns state.tau_masks.T as an O(1) view, retiring the O(n_things · pool_size) _compute_firing_bitmaps rebuild in step()'s hot path; (c) l-hash-consing-as-np-unique in 7.0.7c — wedge-batch dedup replaces the Python seen_in_batch set with np.unique on the canonical keys array, making the Stage 7.0 double-registration bug a shape-level property rather than a maintained invariant.  The three lemmas bind a single dependent-type shape parameter 'pool_size' across axis-1 of the mask matrices and axis-0 of the pool structured array — one shape contract, three arrays, enforceable at load.  Together they enact p-types-are-tensor-shape-obligations on the largest in-memory shapes in the framework, preparing the ground for Stage 7.1's HDF5 compound-dtype schema (l-hdf5-compound-dtypes-mirror-in-memory) which will mirror these in-memory dtypes field-for-field.  No principle changes; all active principles preserved.  Smoke test passes at every substage (854 things, 1087 atoms, 107 oracle pairs; fixed-point trajectories identical to 7.0.6).

title: Stage 7.0.8: oracle_pair_indices + scorer vectorization + dead-code shims retired
body: Stage 7.0.8 enacts l-oracle-pairs-as-index-array and resolves the Stage 7 audit's Severity 1 + Severity 2 findings in one bundle.  (1) oracle_pair_indices: a new cached field on State, built at __init__ as Int[n_pairs, 2] by resolving the authoritative frozenset[frozenset[ThingId]] form against the _id_to_row dict.  Pairs sorted within-pair by (min, max) id-string, making orientation-sensitive scorers deterministic (previously they consumed frozenset iteration order, which was arbitrary in CPython and produced individually nondeterministic counts — though the sum across both orientations was invariant).  (2) state.row_of(tid) / tau_row(tid) / sigma_row(tid): O(1) row accessors backed by a _id_to_row dict cache.  Replace state.things_dict()[tid] for mask-row access — no Thing reconstruction, no per-row copy, no O(n_things · pool_size) rebuild.  (3) Four scorer/objective hot paths (OracleBooleanWitnessTauSigmaScorer, OracleBooleanWitnessSigmaTauScorer, OraclePairsWithWitnessObjective, PoolEntropyMarginalsObjective) vectorized to index tau_masks by oracle_pair_indices and reduce with numpy; no dict(state.things) calls remain in hot paths.  (4) Pool.key_to_bit exposed as a public property; internal call sites no longer reach into Pool._key_to_bit.  (5) Thing.remap removed — State.merge has vectorized the pool-remap as column-permutation since 7.0.7b; remap() had no framework callers post-refactor.  (6) _compute_firing_bitmaps (legacy shim) removed; three smoke-test call sites migrated to firing_bitmaps_of(state, channel).  REWRITE_STAGES.md updated to reflect the actual 7.0/7.1 split and all 7.0.x substages.  Smoke test passes (854 things, 1087 atoms, 107 oracle pairs); new assertions check oracle_pair_indices shape/dtype/range and row_of round-trip consistency.  No principle changes; all active principles preserved.

title: Stage 7.0.9: trajectory → structured-dtype array + aux; Thing docstring realigned with post-7.0.7b role
body: Stage 7.0.9 enacts l-trajectory-as-structured-dtype-array and closes the Stage 7 audit's remaining Severity 2 finding.  (1) TRAJECTORY_DTYPE (7-field structured numpy dtype) becomes the authoritative trajectory representation; field names are part of the type, so typos at write sites fail dtype validation rather than silently returning the .get() default.  max_articulations_per_step uses signed i4 with -1 as the sentinel for Python None — pure numeric, HDF5-ready.  (2) trajectory_aux is a parallel Tuple[dict, ...] carrying optional scorer/objective structural identity per the lemma's counterexample mitigation (split mandatory numeric fields from variable-shape aux fields).  (3) run_to_fixed_point termination check now reads state.trajectory[-1]['articulated_count'] as a structured-array field — no .get() default fallback.  (4) merge uses stable argsort on the 'iteration' field for lock-step sort of structured array + aux tuple; deterministic order.  (5) Legacy tuple-of-dicts trajectory kwarg still accepted at __init__ and normalized at construction.  (6) Thing docstring rewritten: Thing is a VALUE TYPE for standalone construction / row-view reconstruction, NOT the primary storage — State.tau_masks / sigma_masks is authoritative per l-state-things-as-parallel-arrays.  Points callers at state.row_of / tau_row / sigma_row for hot-path access.  Smoke test updated: scorer/objective structural-identity assertions moved from trajectory[-1] to trajectory_aux[-1]; max_articulations_per_step == _MAX_ART_UNBOUNDED_SENTINEL instead of `is None`; new assertion on last.dtype == TRAJECTORY_DTYPE.  Passes end to end (854 things, 1087 atoms, 107 oracle pairs).  No principle changes beyond the addition of p-set-algebra-and-tensor-reduction-use-distinct-forms; all prior active principles preserved.

title: Stage 7.0.10: five scorer classes factored into CellScorer(cells, reduce) compositions
body: Stage 7.0.10 enacts l-scorer-as-shape-contract.  The five original scorer classes (XorOffDiagonalScorer, XorOffDiagonalLogPairScorer, EntropyOfFourCellScorer, OracleBooleanWitnessTauSigmaScorer, OracleBooleanWitnessSigmaTauScorer) collapse to singleton compositions over two orthogonal structural pieces: (a) CellExtractor subclasses (ThingsFourCell, OracleSixteenCell) that map (state, i, j) to a cell count-tensor; (b) Reducer subclasses (SumOffDiagonal, LogProductOffDiagonal, ShannonEntropy, SelectCell(idx)) that reduce a cell tensor to a scalar.  CellScorer(cells, reduce) composes the two; structure tuple recurses as ('scorer', 'cell_scorer', cells.structure, reduce.structure) preserving intensional identity.  OracleSixteenCell exposes all 16 oracle-pair cells — previously 14 were inaccessible without writing a new class; now they cost a SelectCell(idx=...) binding.  Singletons written via functools.partial currying: on_things = partial(CellScorer, ThingsFourCell()); on_oracle_pairs = partial(CellScorer, OracleSixteenCell()); then e.g. scorer_xor_off_diagonal = on_things(SumOffDiagonal()).  Reads 'reducer on domain'; makes the (domain, reduction) factoring visible at the binding site.  Net LOC: -78 for scorers; adding any future scorer in the 2 × 4 Cartesian space now costs a binding, not a class.  Smoke test preserves all previous scorer assertions (tests exercise each singleton directly) and adds a fresh-composition equivalence check proving CellScorer holds no hidden state beyond (cells, reduce).  Composite structure assertion updated to the new ('scorer', 'cell_scorer', ...) format.  Objectives (OraclePairsWithWitnessObjective, PoolEntropyMarginalsObjective) and Composite{Scorer,Objective} unchanged — out of scope.  No principle changes; all active principles preserved.

title: Stage 7.0.11: _count_four_cell vectorized off state.tau_masks; signature takes bit indices
body: Stage 7.0.11 closes the post-7.0.10 Stage 7 audit's Severity 1 regression and Severity 2 K-object-roundtrip.  (1) _count_four_cell's slow path (firing_bitmaps=None) previously iterated state.things, which under post-7.0.7b reconstructs n_things Thing objects with per-row mask copies on every access.  Hot path was fine (step() always provides firing_bitmaps) but any direct scorer call paid O(n_things · pool_size) reconstruction.  7.0.11 replaces the iteration with state.tau_masks[:, i] / [:, j] column slices — O(1) view, no iteration, no Thing reconstruction.  (2) Signature changed from (state, k_i, k_j, channel, firing_bitmaps) to (state, i: int, j: int, *, firing_bitmaps=None, channel='tau').  Callers resolve K → bit_of upfront once; the Protocol-level guard handles missing-K cases before entry.  ThingsFourCell.__call__ drops its state.pool.ks[] roundtrip — it already has the ints.  (3) Four smoke-test callers updated to resolve bit indices upfront.  (4) New smoke-test assertion: slow-path output equals a direct state.tau_masks reference computation, proving the vectorization preserves semantics.  The outer CellScorer.__call__ still accepts K objects (preserves Scorer Protocol); Severity 2's 'step() passes K objects to scorer when it already has ints' remains open — that's a Protocol-level concern touching every Scorer contract and is deferred to a future stage.  Smoke test passes (854 things, 1087 atoms, 107 oracle pairs); all 5 CellScorer singletons bit-identical.  No principle changes; all active principles preserved.

title: Stage 7.0.12: Tier 1 of the S3-cluster — tensor-native S3 + pool orbit fields + State.is_symmetric
body: Stage 7.0.12 enacts Tier 1 of the S3-cluster (l-s3-as-axis-permutation + forward-compat scaffolding for l-k-level-s3-operators + l-combinator-and-s3-operators-are-equivalent) as safe pre-work for Stage 7.1.  Three changes, all pure-refactor or additive: (a) S3.act_on_tsk operates on (3, ...) bool tensors as axis-0 gather; legacy S3.act(TauSigma) rewritten as a thin wrapper — enacts l-s3-as-axis-permutation.  (b) POOL_DTYPE gains orbit_id + orbit_parent (u4 each); for self-orbit K's (all K's under current symmetric-atoms + AND-wedge regime) both equal the own bit index.  Pool.with_k populates the new fields as self-references.  Schema is forward-compatible with Tier 2's Rotated(base, g) K's which will point orbit_id at the base representative.  (c) State.is_symmetric property: True iff every pool K is self-orbit AND tau_masks == sigma_masks.  Not cached (observes no-eager-materialization).  Load-side consumer signal for Stage 7.1 — if True, sigma_masks is derivable from tau_masks on load.  The asymmetric regime is NOT activated; the channel parameter on _count_four_cell / firing_bitmaps_of / Thing.fires is still present and still documented as pre-enactment-scaffold per l-k-level-s3-operators.  Net LOC: +125.  Smoke tests verify S3 identity/transposition/composition/single-cell-tensor-agreement, pool orbit-field presence, and is_symmetric preservation across extract_initial_state + bounded step().  Enacts l-s3-as-axis-permutation.  Prepares l-k-level-s3-operators' pool schema without adding the Rotated K subclass (deferred to Tier 2 = Stage 7.0.13).  No principle changes.

title: Stage 7.0.12 audit fixes: direct S3.act + is_symmetric split into two probes
body: Two Severity-2 findings from the post-7.0.12 audit cleared inline before Tier 2 advancement, so downstream reasoning does not adopt the conflations as canon.  (1) S3.act wrapper regression: the 7.0.12 wrapper went through act_on_tsk by allocating a (3, 1) ndarray — three numpy allocations per single-cell permutation, contradicting the 'no eager materialization' stance.  Restored to a direct Python permutation of the (τ, σ, κ) triple.  l-s3-as-axis-permutation requires act_on_tsk to EXIST as a load-bearing tensor primitive; it does not require the scalar case to round-trip through the tensor form.  Two independent codepaths sharing permutation semantics, each materializing only what its caller needs.  (2) State.is_symmetric conflation split: the 7.0.12 property returned True iff pool had trivial orbits AND sigma_masks == tau_masks, conflating pool structure with mask derivability.  Under Tier 2+ these separate (a pool may have non-trivial Rotated orbits but bitwise-equal masks, or vice versa).  Split into pool_has_trivial_orbits (pure orbit_id == own bit-index query) and sigma_derivable_from_tau (Tier 1 implementation: np.array_equal(σ, τ); Tier 2 will broaden to include orbit-metadata-derivability; Tier 3 may return False even when orbit-derivable if the regime is explicitly asymmetric).  The two probes give Stage 7.1 separate signals: orbit-structure serialization (always needed if non-trivial) vs sigma_masks storage (conditional on derivability).  Keeping the conflated is_symmetric would let Stage 7.1's writer adopt the wrong derivability criterion as canonical.  No lemma enactments change; Tier 1 remains enacted.  Smoke tests updated.

title: Stage 7.0.13: Tier 2 S3-cluster — Rotated K + swap/rotate + pool orbit propagation
body: Stage 7.0.13 enacts l-k-level-s3-operators as Tier 2 scaffolding: the machinery for K-level S3 operations exists and is pool-embeddable with proper orbit-metadata propagation, but the semantic switch (step() / scorers / extract_initial_state articulating Rotated K's) is NOT thrown — that's Tier 3.  (a) New Rotated(base, perm) K subclass alongside Atom/Wedge/ZeroK: grade preserved, structure = ('rotated', base.structure(), perm.perm), key() JSON-round-trippable per p-bijective-hash-consing, atoms() unchanged (rotation doesn't introduce atoms), normalize() collapses identity-rotation/nested-rotations/zero-rotation cases.  (b) Top-level helpers swap(k) and rotate(k, g).  (c) Pool.with_k detects Rotated and propagates orbit metadata: base is added first (recursive); new row's orbit_id inherits base's orbit_id (root), orbit_parent points at base's bit_index (immediate).  Atom/Wedge/ZeroK paths unchanged (self-orbit).  (d) Smoke tests verify inductive K properties, swap involution, rotation composition via S3.__mul__, ZeroK fixed point, pool orbit bookkeeping (adding swap(atom) creates 2 pool entries with correct orbit linkage), and State.pool_has_trivial_orbits correctly reports False when a Rotated K is in the pool.  Not in scope (deferred to Tier 3 = post-Stage-7.1): State-level mask population for Rotated K's (currently zero-padded via State.with_pool); any use of Rotated in step()/scorers/extract_initial_state; activation of the asymmetric regime (d-wedge-combinator-general-exterior-algebra remains dormant).  Doc fix included: REWRITE_STAGES.md 7.0.12 entry updated to reflect the split probes instead of the retired is_symmetric.

title: Stage 7.0.13 supplement: compose() added + wedge-normalize generalized for Rotated / ZeroK leaves
body: Closes the post-7.0.13 audit's Severity 2 finding — l-k-level-s3-operators's body lists three K-level primitives (swap, rotate, compose); the initial 7.0.13 enactment omitted compose.  Supplement adds it inline to keep the same-stage enactment complete.  (a) compose(k1, k2, g) = Wedge(k1, rotate(k2, g)).normalize() — produces one cross-orientation term of the general exterior-algebra combinator; the full combinator under l-combinator-and-s3-operators-are-equivalent is (compose(k1, k2, (τσ)) + compose(k2, k1, (τσ))) in GF(2) with AND semantics.  (b) _flatten_wedge_leaves and Wedge.normalize generalized to handle non-Atom leaves.  Leaves now include Atom, ZeroK, and Rotated — any non-Wedge K.  Rotated(A, non-identity) is a distinct leaf from A under intensional keys, so Wedge(A, swap(A)) is correctly NOT nilpotent while Wedge(A, A) still is.  Nilpotency check switched from distinct-atom count to distinct-leaf count (the two coincided under pre-7.0.13 atom-only wedges but diverge once Rotated leaves appear).  (c) Zero absorption bug fixed: previously _flatten silently dropped ZeroK leaves, making Wedge(A, ZeroK).normalize() incorrectly return A (violating A ∧ 0 = 0).  Latent — live call paths in step() filter ZeroK before wedge articulation, so Wedge(K, ZeroK) was never constructed — but it surfaced via compose(A, ZeroK, g) in the new smoke test.  Fix: include ZeroK leaves in the flattened tuple and check is_zero() in Wedge.normalize; the quotient now matches d-wedge-combinator-general-exterior-algebra + d-wedge-bit-and-of-parents semantics.  Smoke tests added: compose equivalence with explicit Wedge+rotate construction, compose with identity S3 element, compose(A, ZeroK, g) == ZeroK, atom-set union.  All pre-existing wedge tests preserved bit-identical.  No lemma-status change (l-k-level-s3-operators enacted in 7.0.13 main commit; this supplement completes the enactment).

title: Stage 7.1: hybrid JSONL + HDF5 I/O — dump_state / load_state; enacts l-hdf5-compound-dtypes-mirror-in-memory
body: Stage 7.1 delivers the originally-planned Stage 7 content: durable dump_state / load_state for the full State.  Storage split per p-storage-matches-data-shape: small-algebra authoritative forms to JSONL (pool.jsonl, things.jsonl, oracle_pairs.jsonl, weights.jsonl, trajectory_aux.jsonl); bulk tensor caches to HDF5 (state.h5 with /masks/tau, optional /masks/sigma, /trajectory as compound dtype mirroring TRAJECTORY_DTYPE).  Sigma masks conditional: omitted when state.sigma_derivable_from_tau (the two probes from Stage 7.0.12 give the writer independent signals); loader reconstructs σ = τ.copy() when absent.  Schema version attribute (7.1.0) guards load-time compatibility.  Shape invariants checked on load: pool_size matches pool.jsonl row count AND tau_masks axis-1; n_things matches things.jsonl row count AND tau_masks axis-0.  New module-level function k_from_structure(struct) → K closes the p-bijective-hash-consing round-trip: json.loads(k.key()) → k_from_structure → k' where k' == k intensionally; loader re-encodes and asserts key equality per-row as a bijection witness.  Smoke test: dump_state → load_state → assert loaded.signature() == original.signature() + trajectory/aux/iteration equality + /masks/sigma confirmed absent in HDF5 for current symmetric state (forward-compat with Tier 3).  Enacts l-hdf5-compound-dtypes-mirror-in-memory.  Nine of ten lemmas now enacted; only l-combinator-and-s3-operators-are-equivalent remains active (Tier 3 scope).  No principle changes; all active principles preserved.

title: Stage 7.1.1: pool migrated to HDF5; chunked + extensible datasets; algebraic self-validation via K-structure
body: Closes the post-7.1 audit's three Severity-2 architectural divergences and the Severity-3 validation gaps in one stage.  (a) JSONL retirement direction: the post-7.1 audit surfaced that d-disk-as-merge-protocol's append-only-JSONL framing was being departed from; the user ratified the divergence with the directive to migrate AWAY from JSONL entirely.  Stage 7.1.1 takes the first step: pool migrates from pool.jsonl to /pool/keys_array as an HDF5 compound dataset.  Remaining JSONL files (things, oracle_pairs, weights, trajectory_aux) are explicitly transitional — direction is full retirement.  (b) Lemma alignment to l-hdf5-compound-dtypes-mirror-in-memory: pool now lives in HDF5 with POOL_HDF5_DTYPE = [(key, S512), (grade, u1), (orbit_id, u4), (orbit_parent, u4)] mirroring the in-memory POOL_DTYPE field-for-field with the 'key' field widened to fixed-width bytes.  Truncation risk (S512) is guarded at dump time; future migration to variable-length strings or key decomposition is the planned escape valve.  pool_size attribute mirrored across HDF5 root, /pool group, /masks group; load asserts all three agree — the lemma's 'shape contract enforced across two arrays' invariant.  (c) Chunked + extensible HDF5 datasets: every dataset created with chunks=True + maxshape supporting axis-0 growth (mask matrices also extensible on axis-1).  Readiness for Stage-10 parallel-shard accumulation that extends prior dumps without re-dumping; no behavior change today.  (d) Algebraic self-validation per the audit's S3/Fano framing: replaces explicit range-checks on orbit_id with derive-from-K-structure consistency check via _expected_orbit_id_for(k, pool, own_bit) — walks the Rotated chain to the orbit root and computes pool.bit_of(root); mismatch with stored orbit_id catches both bounds errors and structure/orbit inconsistencies that a range-check alone would miss.  Trajectory iteration field validated for consecutive monotonicity via np.array_equal against np.arange(1, n_iter+1) — single algebraic check covers gaps, duplicates, miscounts.  Trajectory shape consistency with n_iter attribute also asserted.  Empty-state round-trip added (n_things=0, pool_size=0, n_iter=0) — the only Sev-3 item not covered by algebraic self-validation, fixed via explicit edge-case smoke test.  SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 7.1.1.  No principle changes; lemma-status unchanged (l-hdf5-compound-dtypes-mirror-in-memory remains enacted, now more fully).  Direction note for d-disk-as-merge-protocol: the append-only multi-shard model is superseded by truncate-per-dump + State.merge-pre-dump for the symmetric / Tier-1+2 regime; multi-shard incremental accumulation via HDF5 chunked-extensible datasets becomes available when Stage 10 parallel shards land.

title: d-wedge-combinator-general-exterior-algebra σ-formula: strict GF(2), symmetric-atom degeneration is τ_K=σ_K=0
body: The decision body's claim 'Under τ = σ atoms this degenerates (τ_K = 0, σ_K = τ_A·τ_B)' is a typo.  Under strict GF(2) arithmetic applied consistently to both the τ and σ formulas, symmetric atoms degenerate to BOTH τ_K = 0 AND σ_K = 0 (consistent with exterior algebra's u ∧ u = 0).  The original body's 'σ_K = τ_A·τ_B' would require either (a) a different arithmetic for σ (boolean OR / max rather than GF(2)) — which would make the algebra non-uniform and conflict with p-kappa-is-derived-xor's GF(2) assumption — or (b) a computational shortcut that was over-zealously applied.  Strict GF(2) is the consistent reading.  Implication: meaningful asymmetric wedges under the general combinator require at least one Rotated parent (since symmetric atoms produce identically-zero wedges).  This reading is what the Stage 7.1.2 / Tier 3 activation depends on — _articulate_wedges_batch invokes the general combinator only when at least one leaf is Rotated; all-symmetric wedges fall back to d-wedge-bit-and-of-parents' AND semantics.  The decision's active status is preserved; this correction clarifies the body's intended algebra.

title: Stage 7.1.2: Tier 3 — general combinator activation on Rotated-parent wedges; final lemma enacted
body: Stage 7.1.2 enacts l-combinator-and-s3-operators-are-equivalent as the minimum-viable Tier 3 activation of the S3-cluster.  10/10 lemmas now enacted.  (1) Sev 3 (c) inline fix from the post-7.1.1 audit: new smoke-test block exercises a Pool containing an Atom + its swap through dump/load, verifies orbit metadata (orbit_id pointing at base, orbit_parent pointing at base) round-trips and pool_has_trivial_orbits correctly reports False after load.  (2) _articulate_wedges_batch gains a Rotated-parent detection branch: when at least one leaf of a candidate grade-2 Wedge is a Rotated K, the wedge's (τ, σ) are computed via the strict-GF(2) general exterior-algebra combinator (τ_W = (τ_A·σ_B) ⊕ (σ_A·τ_B); σ_W = (τ_A·τ_B) ⊕ (σ_A·σ_B)) instead of AND-of-parents.  For all-non-Rotated wedges (the current case everywhere step() articulates), the AND path remains per d-wedge-bit-and-of-parents' default.  Higher-grade wedges with Rotated leaves fall back to AND conservatively (grade-k recursive formula deferred until a use case demands).  σ firing bitmaps sourced via O(1) state.sigma_masks.T view — no eager materialization.  (3) End-to-end smoke test: rotate two atoms by (τκ) transposition (S3((2, 1, 0))); manually construct an asymmetric State; articulate Wedge(rot_x, rot_y) via the general combinator; verify the resulting wedge has τ=0, σ=1 on the test thing; verify state.sigma_derivable_from_tau==False; dump → verify /masks/sigma dataset is present with sigma_stored=True attribute; load → verify asymmetric firing preserved bit-identically.  First smoke test to produce σ ≠ τ on any K and trigger genuine /masks/sigma storage rather than derivation.  (4) SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 7.2.0 (major stays 7 so 7.1.x dumps still load).  Depends on c-d-wedge-combinator-typo-fix for the strict-GF(2) reading that licenses the implementation choice.  Non-goals: step()-level demand-driven Rotated articulation; grade-3+ recursive combinator; further JSONL retirement.

title: Stage 7.2: post-Tier-3 step()-level asymmetric regime activation; closes two planning questions
body: Resolves q-post-tier3-demand-criterion-for-rotated and q-step-co-fire-enumeration-multi-channel as a single stage (per user's recommended scope).  Three pieces: (1) step() Phase 1 co-fire enumeration extended to τ∪σ union — under symmetric regime identical to τ-only co-fire; under asymmetric regime Rotated K's (τ=0 but σ=1) now participate in pair discovery.  firing_bitmaps passed to _articulate_wedges_batch stays τ-only (AND-wedge path uses τ-firing; general-combinator path reads σ separately from state.sigma_masks.T).  (2) articulate_rotated_from_residue(state, top_n=5): plateau-triggered orbit seeding.  For oracle pairs whose Boolean-earning witness is missing (either off-diagonal cell empty across pool K's), identifies K's that discriminate (fire on exactly one of the pair), ranks by unearned-pair count, articulates (τκ) and (σκ) S3-orbit members of the top-N.  Base K's only (already-Rotated K's skipped — keeps orbit tree depth 1 per pass).  New Rotated K's enter pool with correct firing columns via _rotated_firing_columns helper (S3 axis permutation on base's tsk tensor) — not zero-padded.  (3) run_to_asymmetric_fixed_point(state, ...): step → plateau → orbit-seed → step ... until seeding finds no residue OR max_plateau_cycles (default 32) safety-valve trips.  Bootstrapping: under symmetric atoms the first-iteration σ/τ residue is identical across all K's (can't distinguish residue direction), so the orbit seeding's articulation IS the seed that breaks symmetry — after the first call, general combinator can produce asymmetric wedges in subsequent step() iterations.  Smoke test: 2-atom state with un-earned oracle pair → 4 Rotated K's articulated → extended co-fire finds pairs → general combinator produces asymmetric wedge (τ=0, σ=1 on shared thing) → sigma_derivable_from_tau flips False.  All pre-existing smoke-test paths preserved bit-identical.  No schema change (SCHEMA_VERSION stays 7.2.0).  Non-goals: grade-3+ Rotated wedges (NotImplementedError guard from 7.1.2 follow-up stays); automatic σ-channel scorer invocation in run_to_asymmetric_fixed_point; further JSONL retirement.

title: Stage 7.2.1: orbit-seed events recorded as minimal (base_bit, perm) algebraic datum in trajectory
body: Closes the post-7.2 audit's Severity 2 finding: articulate_rotated_from_residue was silently mutating state without leaving a trajectory trace — violating p-closed-self-referential-loop.  Fixed by appending a structured trajectory row + aux entry per orbit-seed invocation.  The aux entry carries a list of {base_bit, perm} records — the MINIMAL algebraic datum from which a replayer can deterministically reconstruct the post-seed state.  Per the user's reframe: each (base, perm) record represents a packed S3 node carrying 24 latent (S3 × 4-cell Belnap) configurations per thing — recording just the originating orientation is sufficient; the other orientations are derivable.  The trajectory becomes an event log aligned with the event-sourcing / CQRS direction: state = fold(events, initial_state).  step() aux entries now carry source='step' for symmetry.  Smoke test verifies: (1) trajectory entry count, (2) aux 'source' tag + events list, (3) deterministic reconstruction proof — pre-seed pool + events list → reconstructed pool == actual post-seed pool key-by-key.  Restores p-closed-self-referential-loop: every state mutation is visible in the trajectory log.

title: Stage 7.2.2: lazy syndrome-decoded orbit demands; (τ⊕σ)|σ = τ|σ eliminates virtual matmul
body: Supersedes c-stage-7-2-post-tier3-step-asymmetric-activation and c-stage-7-2-1-orbit-seed-trajectory-events.  The eager write-path orbit model (articulate_rotated_from_residue, run_to_asymmetric_fixed_point, Pool.with_k_and_orbit, _rotated_firing_columns) is replaced by a syndrome-decoded approach inline in step() Phase 2.  Key insight: under the F₂² constraint, (τ⊕σ)|σ = τ|σ algebraically — the τ∪σ union firing of any S3-rotated K equals the base K's τ∪σ.  The virtual 3P×3P co-fire matmul was 9 identical copies of the base P×P block: zero new co-fire pairs from orbit expansion.  What orbit expansion DID add was new demanded wedges: for each base candidate pair (i, j), also demand Wedge(K_i, Rotated(K_j, g)) for the (τκ) and (σκ) S3 generators (grade-1 K's only, to avoid grade-3+ Rotated-leaf constructions the general combinator can't yet handle).  These demands are inferred from the base co-fire without materializing virtual firing rows — the algebraic identity IS the syndrome; we decode without computing.  Eager functions purged; their smoke-test blocks removed; r-eager-orbit-seeding records the rejected path.  Grade-3+ Rotated-leaf wedges continue to be skipped (not crashed): uncapturable demand doesn't remove pool entries, so Tarski convergence is preserved.  The canonical orbit model is now: base P×P matmul + syndrome-decoded Rotated-leaf demands inline in step() Phase 2.  No separate orbit-seeding event; no eager mask materialization; no plateau detection; no max_plateau_cycles.  Resolves the post-7.2 audit's Sev 2 (dead code carrying stale semantics) and the Tarski correction (max_plateau_cycles as crash condition).

title: Stage 7.3: fire_pair as canonical computation; pool = computation graph; masks = cache
body: Stage 7.3 introduces State.fire_pair(k: K) → (tau_col, sigma_col) as the single canonical computation pathway for all K types.  The pool is reframed as a computation graph (K structures describe HOW to compute firing); the mask matrix is reframed as a cache (atom columns stored by extract; wedge columns cached on articulation via fire_pair).  fire_pair dispatches on K type: Atom → direct stored-column read; Rotated → S3 axis-permutation on fire_pair(base); Wedge with all-atom leaves → AND-of-parents (d-wedge-bit-and-of-parents); Wedge with Rotated leaves → general combinator right-fold / shingling (grade-k GF(2) cross-term formula); ZeroK → all-False.  Memoized per k.key() on the State instance.  Temporary-guarded fold (both tau_new and sig_new computed from OLD accumulator values).  _articulate_wedges_batch delegates entirely to fire_pair for degeneracy checks and column computation — ~100 LOC simpler than the pre-7.3 dual-path implementation.  The AND/combinator dispatch is transparent to callers.  Evolves l-state-things-as-parallel-arrays: the stacked mask matrix is no longer THE primary representation but a CACHE of the primary representation (pool structure + atom columns).  The full primary representation is: pool K-tree structures (computation graph) + atom mask columns (ground truth) + fire_pair (evaluation function).  Opens the path to l-belnap-encoded-mask-matrix which would make the cache's gap-vs-known-False distinction explicit at the type level.
